title: Define the canonical state machine and continuation rules
goal: Translate the plan-defined canonical status model, continuation rules, next_action precedence, and minimal checkpoint event set into fully traceable implementation-facing documentation.
scope: Define the allowed canonical statuses and their meanings exactly as written in plan.md.
Define the rule that next_action is the primary continuation dispatcher when valid.
Define the minimal canonical checkpoint event set and the limited role of optional progress events.
Define the high-level transition model and repair-loop allowances exactly as written in plan.md.
out_of_scope: Changing identity-allocation behavior that belongs to p2-t01.
Changing persistence schema details that belong to p1-t03.
Inventing extra lifecycle states or extra canonical checkpoint events beyond plan.md.
expected_outputs: A state-machine specification derived strictly from plan.md.
A checkpoint-event specification derived strictly from plan.md.
A traceability matrix linking continuation rules to exact plan sections and runtime review targets.
Implementation-facing guidance for how ops_* helper contracts should preserve explicit checkpoint ordering and valid next_action-first continuation behavior.
A completed ticket artifact at docs/v0.6.0/p1-t02-spec.md whose source usage is consistent with the ticket inputs.
acceptance_criteria: The canonical status set matches plan.md exactly.
The continuation rules explicitly preserve next_action precedence over status-derived heuristics when next_action is valid.
The checkpoint-event contract does not require commit events when no repository commit is attempted.
The artifact remains strictly derived from plan.md and does not invent new canonical semantics.

When ops_* helper contracts are updated in downstream implementation work, they reference docs/v0.6.0/ops-tool-contract-alignment-plan.md for checkpoint-ordering and next_action-precedence alignment without introducing extra canonical states or events.
The completed p1-t02 artifact explicitly checks that the ticket inputs are complete, non-contradictory, and sufficient for the ticket scope.

forbidden_moves: Do not elevate optional progress events into required canonical continuation checkpoints.
Do not let status override a valid canonical next_action.
